text: I arrive as parent evaluating whether the offer is serious. My past: I have seen many learning products overpromise, so I look for safety, support, and realistic outcomes.
Today I want to: Decide whether the product feels credible enough to discuss further.
My life goal is: Help my family make education decisions that create real options.
I have 100 life-years. Each meaningful action costs 1 month(s); each committed dollar costs 1 year(s).

I think: This sounds ambitious, but the claim that it can replace school is too big for me to accept from a hero section alone.
My reaction: I like the promise of less planning and personalization, but the wording feels sales-heavy and makes me cautious.
Idea: Show a real sample week for one child, including mentor support, parent oversight, and what happens when the child gets stuck.
Life-cost decision: Spending 1 month of life is only worth it if the next screen gives concrete proof that this could create real education options for my family.
Truth assessment: My honest judgment is that Self Degree is interesting but not yet credible enough for a serious family discussion; confidence 0.72.
